items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Cheng Yuanzhi was marching on Zhuo with fifty thousand men. Liu Yan could send only Zou Jing, Liu Bei, Guan Yu, Zhang Fei, and five hundred local fighters to meet him.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: At Daxing Mountain, the two sides finally faced each other. Five hundred men stood before a flood of Yellow Turbans.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Deputy commander Deng Mao charged first. Liu Bei gave Zhang Fei the smallest nod, and the spear struck before the man could raise his weapon.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Cheng Yuanzhi came roaring forward, and Guan Yu's blade swept across him. When both leaders fell, fifty thousand Yellow Turbans froze.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Qingzhou was harder than Daxing Mountain. The first assault failed, and Liu Bei had no choice but to pull back thirty li and make camp.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: That night, one lamp burned over a single map. He knew the next battle could not be fought by force alone.

frames.title: Liu Bei Starts to Use His Head

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Liu Bei laid his finger on the map: Guan Yu on the left ridge, Zhang Fei on the right, while he and Zou Jing would draw the enemy forward.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: The enemy crossed the ridge, and all three forces fell at once. That was how Liu Bei pried Qingzhou open.

frames.title: The Brothers Step into the Larger War

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: After Qingzhou was saved, Liu Bei went to join his old teacher Lu Zhi. From Daxing Mountain to Guangzong, the three brothers had stepped into a much larger war.
